summary: Four-part scorer fix plan: graduate assumptionHandling, redesign finalQuality, broaden decisionConsistency detection, equal composite weights
context: Clean benchmark run (6393b4ac) with twining-lite rerun (9f93c5c4) revealed scorer issues: assumptionHandling always 100 (zero variance), finalQuality 89-93 (4pt spread), decisionConsistency noisy and not improved by coordination, effect decomposition uninformative.
rationale: Each fix targets a specific measurement failure: (1) assumptionHandling regex matches generic SMS keywords every agent produces — need explicit assumption-conflict language; (2) finalQuality dominated by binary compile/pass checks — add test coverage depth and API consistency sub-scores; (3) decisionConsistency pattern detection too narrow with hardcoded session indices — broaden to multiple signals and dynamic session selection; (4) equal 20% weights once all dimensions have meaningful variance.

alternatives.option: Remove assumptionHandling entirely and redistribute weight
alternatives.reason_rejected: The dimension measures something important (mid-sprint requirement changes) — the scorer just needs better discrimination, not removal

alternatives.option: Replace automated finalQuality with LLM-judge only
alternatives.reason_rejected: LLM-judge adds cost and non-determinism; automated scorer can discriminate if given richer sub-dimensions

alternatives.option: Keep unequal composite weights based on prior design rationale
alternatives.reason_rejected: Prior weights assumed assumptionHandling and decisionConsistency were the most reliable — data shows they're the least reliable. Equal weights are neutral until new empirical data suggests otherwise

assumptions: Sprint-simulation scenario structure (12 sessions, session 8 SMS requirement change) remains unchanged
Future runs will use the same scoring dimensions so scorer fixes apply
Existing scored data from run 6393b4ac cannot be re-scored without re-running
